subtitle: Olivine winnowed from a 49,000-year-old eroding cone, reached only by a 2.5-mile walk from the southernmost point of the United States.

levers.photo_tone: olive-green sand, ochre cone walls, hard south-Hawaii light

spike_statement: The green is olivine, winnowed by the sea from a 49,000-year-old cone that is still eroding into the bay it half-encloses.
